channel.title: News — Rendeiro Lab
channel.description: Recent news from the Rendeiro Lab

item.title: Ariadna awarded the DOC Fellowship of the Austrian Academy of Sciences
item.guid: Ariadna awarded the DOC Fellowship of the Austrian Academy of Sciences2026-07-04
item.description: Ariadna Villanueva Marijuan has been awarded the DOC Fellowship (Doctoral Fellowship Program) of the Austrian Academy of Sciences (ÖAW). The DOC fellowship is a highly competitive program that supports outstanding doctoral candidates across all areas of basic research, enabling them to focus on their dissertation with dedicated funding. Ariadna is the first PhD student in the lab to receive a fellowship: a major achievement! We are all proud of you Ariadna! Congratulations!

item.title: Ariadna, Ernesto and Shrestha attended the Ascona Workshop 2026
item.guid: Ariadna, Ernesto and Shrestha attended the Ascona Workshop 20262026-07-01
item.description: Ariadna, Ernesto and Shrestha attended the Ascona Workshop: Statistical and AI methods for multi-modal multi-scale modeling of biological systems at Monte Verità, Ascona, Switzerland. The workshop (28 June - 3 July 2026) addressed current challenges in integrating and interpreting multi-modal datasets across biological scales, bringing together leading researchers in statistics, machine learning, systems biology and biomedicine. Thank you to the organizers Niko Beerenwinkel, Valentina Boeva, Pet

item.title: Marsilea spotlighted at GitHub maintainer month!
item.guid: Marsilea spotlighted at GitHub maintainer month!2026-05-01
item.description: Marsilea, our framework for composable visualizations has been selected for a spotlight on Github Maintainer Month . The feature contains an interview with Yimin, who conceived and maintains the project. Open source runs a large fraction of the world's digital infrastructure, but this is thanks to the largely unrewarded efforts of open source maintainers worldwide. Thank you to the Github Community Management team for the opportunity to celebrate maintainers of open source software.

item.title: Lab dinner
item.guid: Lab dinner2026-04-24
item.description: We had a very nice dinner together at 'In Dish', an Indian restaurant in Vienna's first district. The food was delicious, the spirits high, and it was great to see so many people together. Thanks Lisa for the restaurant suggestion, Samir for organizing, and everyone for joining!

item.title: We took part in the Vienna City Marathon
item.guid: We took part in the Vienna City Marathon2026-04-19
item.description: For the first time our team ran together in the Vienna City Marathon relay. CeMM was the proud sponsor of the race and it was great to meet and run with colleagues from the institute! Yimin, Lisa, Tamas and Andre together ran the 42 km in 4 hours and 11 minutes. We finished with a nice photo at the MuseumsQuartier - thanks everyone for joining and doing your best!

item.title: Samir and Yimin took part in the 2026 SpatialData hackathon
item.guid: Samir and Yimin took part in the 2026 SpatialData hackathon2026-04-15
item.description: The event held in Padova, Italy over 4 days focused on SpatialData, the basic data structure underlying spatially resolved data within the scverse ecosystem. SpatialData also underlies WSIData, the data object LazySlide uses for whole slide image analysis. The hackathon had an ambitious program , with themes including design modernization, bioconducted compatibility, and visualization. Samir and Yimin focused both on SpatialData design and its modernization. Thank you to the organizers and to th

item.title: Andre elected to the Young Academy of the Austrian Academy of Sciences
item.guid: Andre elected to the Young Academy of the Austrian Academy of Sciences2026-03-26
item.description: Andre has been elected as a member of the Young Academy of the Austrian Academy of Sciences (ÖAW) . The Young Academy brings together excellent early-career scientists from diverse disciplines to contribute to the Austrian scientific landscape and engage in interdisciplinary exchange and outreach. We are honored by this recognition and look forward to contributing to the Academy's mission.

item.title: LazySlide is now published in Nature Methods!
item.guid: LazySlide is now published in Nature Methods!2026-03-25
item.description: Our framework for accessible and interoperable whole slide image analysis is now published in Nature Methods . This work was led by Yimin Zheng, who developed the core architecture enabling scverse-compatible analysis of histopathological images at scale. LazySlide brings the interoperability and efficiency of single-cell genomics workflows to computational pathology, lowering barriers for researchers across disciplines. Check out the documentation at lazyslide.readthedocs.io and the code at Git

item.title: Our lab received the Beyond the Journal Award for Open Science!
item.guid: Our lab received the Beyond the Journal Award for Open Science!2026-02-26
item.description: We are honored to receive the Experiment Foundation 's Beyond the Journal Award, recognizing scientists pushing the boundaries of open science. The award acknowledges our commitment to transparency through our publicly hosted , open-source lab manual that makes internal workflows and computational practices reusable by the community. We are grateful for this recognition and remain committed to embedding reproducibility into our daily research culture. Read more about the award and the other awar

item.title: Clemens joins the lab!
item.guid: Clemens joins the lab!2026-01-19
item.description: Clemens Kohl joins the lab as a postdoctoral researcher. After studying Food Science and Biotechnology in Vienna, he did a Master at ETH Zürich in Basel, Switzerland, and a PhD in Bioinformatics at the Max Planck Institute for Molecular Genetics in Berlin, Germany. With a strong background in single-cell analysis and clustering methods the lab will gain a major bridge to the genomics and single-cell field, as well as a close connection to the group of Georg Busslinger at the Medical University o

item.title: Congratulations to Lisa on securing a postdoctoral fellowship!
item.guid: Congratulations to Lisa on securing a postdoctoral fellowship!2026-01-12
item.description: Lisa Kleissl has been awarded the prestigious APART-MINT fellowship from the Austrian Academy of Sciences (ÖAW). This competitive fellowship supports outstanding early-career researchers in the natural sciences, technology, and medicine and will support her work for one year. Congratulations Lisa on this well-deserved recognition of your ideas and research!

item.title: StainX: CUDA-optimized stain normalization is now open source!
item.guid: StainX: CUDA-optimized stain normalization is now open source!2025-12-06
item.description: We are excited to release StainX , a Python package for enhanced stain normalization of histopathology images with batch processing support. StainX provides multiple algorithms (Reinhard, Macenko, Histogram Matching) with automatic backend selection for CPU, GPU (CUDA), and Apple Silicon (MPS) devices. Our preliminary benchmarks show 8-11x speedups compared to standard PyTorch implementations when using optimized CUDA kernels! Install it with pip install stainx and check out the GitHub repositor

item.title: Welcome to Simon!
item.guid: Welcome to Simon!2025-12-01
item.description: Simon Schindler joined the lab as a joint PhD student between our lab and the lab of Joerg Menche at the LBI-NetMed. He has a background in electrical engineering and machine learning and will work on geometric learning of cellular organization.

item.title: Ernesto presented his work at Cytodata 2025
item.guid: Ernesto presented his work at Cytodata 20252025-11-21
item.description: Ernesto Abila attented the Cytodata 2025 hackathon and symposium which took place at Campus Berlin-Buch, Berlin, Germany. He presented a posted on his main PhD project as well as a poster on our framework for whole slide image analysis - LazySlide. Thank you to the organizers for putting together such an exciting event!

item.title: Andre gave a talk at the 2nd European CyTOF conference
item.guid: Andre gave a talk at the 2nd European CyTOF conference2025-11-20
item.description: Andre gave a keynote talk on 'Leveraging spatial biology insights across anatomical scales' at the 2nd European CyTOF conference which took place in Vienna It was a very nice conference full of exciting use cases of high-dimensional and spatial data as well as nice applications of it. Thank you for the organizers, in particular Lena Mueller for the invitation.

item.title: LazySlide was showcased at the 2025 scverse conference
item.guid: LazySlide was showcased at the 2025 scverse conference2025-11-19
item.description: Yimin and Tamas presented a community showcase of LazySlide - our framework for accessible and interoperable whole slide image analysis in the 2025 scverse conference. We are eager to support the scverse community in the analysis of histopathological images and to receive feedback on LazySlide. Thank you to the organizers for selecting LazySlide for the showcase and thank you to Yimin and Tamas for giving the workshop at late night hours!

item.title: Samir joins the lab!
item.guid: Samir joins the lab!2025-11-03
item.description: Samir Moustafa joins the lab as a data scientist With a strong background in graph neural networks and the development of efficient solutions in deep learning, the lab will gain major experience in the fast developing field. We are happy Samir decided to join the team and wish him an exciting and successfull time in the lab - welcome!

item.title: We joined the 2025 CeMM halloween party!
item.guid: We joined the 2025 CeMM halloween party!2025-10-31
item.description: Following a long-standing tradition at CeMM, we joined the 2025 CeMM halloween party which this year was themed "Dumb ways to die". This year we performed a little theater play with a commentary on the current publication system, and we won the prize for best performance! Thank you to the first year PhD students for organizing such a fun party and to all the staff for making this cool night possible!

item.title: Andre attended ESSB 2025
item.guid: Andre attended ESSB 20252025-10-17
item.description: Andre attended the 2nd meeting of the European Society for Spatial Biology in Heidelberg, Germany. It was great to see the rapid advances in spatial biology and meet friends and colleagues in the field. The field is full of exciting research and we are looking forward to contributing to it! Thank you to the organizers for a great meeting!

item.title: The LBI-NetMed had its first SAB meeting
item.guid: The LBI-NetMed had its first SAB meeting2025-10-07
item.description: The first meeting of the LBI-NetMed scientific advisory board took place on October 7th at the institute. The SAB is composed by Joseph Loscalzo, Katy Börner, Marc van Kempen, and Marinka Zitnik - all very accomplished researchers and leaders in their fields. We are very happy to have them provide advice on our research and institute organization. Thank you also to the Rendeiro lab members who presented, Ernesto Abila, Parijat Chakraborty and Shrestha Srivastava. Especially Parijat and Shrestha

item.title: Andre participated in a panel discussion at FotoWien
item.guid: Andre participated in a panel discussion at FotoWien2025-10-05
item.description: Andre participated in a panel discussion on the intersection of art and science as part of FotoWien, Vienna's international photography festival. The discussion was entitled "Medical Gaze and In/visibility" and was moderated by Monika Pietrzak-Franger, professor at the University of Vienna, head of the department of English and American Studies, and specializing in historical illness iconography, knowledge production and injustice. The panel also included artist and researcher Fiona Tang, who br

item.title: We participated in the 20th run for cancer research
item.guid: We participated in the 20th run for cancer research2025-10-04
item.description: We took part in the run for cancer research in which altogether a combined distance of 46,239 laps were run, each with a donation to support cancer research. With this we hope to not only raise funds for cancer research in Austria, but also awareness about cancer, and promote its prevention.

item.title: We took part in the 2025 CeMM outing
item.guid: We took part in the 2025 CeMM outing2025-10-03
item.description: The CeMM outing is a day where the science meets society in the physical space. This year, we visited the historic and just renovated Theater an der Wien - the perfect setting for the 2025 theme of "changing the scenery".

item.title: Andre gave a talk at the 2025 OeGMBT meeting
item.guid: Andre gave a talk at the 2025 OeGMBT meeting2025-09-24
item.description: Andre gave a talk at the 2025 annual meeting of the Austrian Society for Molecular Biology and Biotechnology (OeGMBT) in Innsbruck, Austria. It was great to see the diverse research made by the local community, in particular by the young researchers who presented their work. Thank you to the organizers and in particular to Markus Mandl for the invitation!

item.title: We took part in the 2025 LBI-NetMed retreat
item.guid: We took part in the 2025 LBI-NetMed retreat2025-09-16
item.description: The LBI-NetMed had its yearly retreat in Horn, Austria. Andre, Parijat and Shrestha represented our lab and had a great time discussing science, networking, and thinking on the future of the institute. Thank you to the organizers for a great retreat!
